description_long: This object is an SEL Feeder Protection Relay control cabinet, commonly used in electrical substations and power distribution systems. The front panel features a digital display, multiple labeled buttons, and indicator lights for system status and control, with clear manufacturer branding from Schweitzer Engineering Laboratories. The sides are solid and unadorned, while the rear reveals a complex array of terminal blocks, wiring, and connectors, indicating its function as an interface for multiple electrical circuits. The cabinet is constructed primarily from metal, with plastic and rubber components for buttons, connectors, and insulation. Its robust, boxy form is designed for durability and secure mounting in technical environments. The overall finish is dark, with a mix of matte and slightly glossy surfaces.
description: A robust, box-shaped SEL Feeder Protection Relay control cabinet with a digital display, labeled buttons, and a complex rear wiring interface, primarily made of metal and plastic.
description_view_0: Front view showing the digital display, labeled buttons, indicator lights, and manufacturer branding.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the solid, featureless metal panel and the depth of the cabinet.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing terminal blocks, wiring, connectors, and internal components for electrical interfacing.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the solid metal panel and protruding rear connectors.
